channel.title: NPR Topics: Environment
channel.description: Breaking news on the environment, climate change, pollution, and endangered species. Also featuring Climate Connections, a special series on climate change co-produced by NPR and National Geographic.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
item.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

(Image credit: Andy Wong/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The oldest known recording of a whale song reveals how oceans have changed
item.description: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.content:encoded: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Moss is an underdog in the world of plants when it comes to federal protection
item.description: Hundreds of plants are protected by the Endangered Species Act, but moss is an underdog. There's only one type that has federal protection, but a group of scientists wants to change that.
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of plants are protected by the Endangered Species Act, but moss is an underdog. There's only one type that has federal protection, but a group of scientists wants to change that.
item.dc:creator: Kate Grumke

item.title: Bringing marine life back to South Florida's 'forgotten edge'
item.description: Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.
item.content:encoded:
Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.

(Image credit: Nathan Rott)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Easy-to-use solar panels are coming, but utilities are trying to delay them
item.description: Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.
item.content:encoded:
Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: Making wastewater drinkable is a growing trend as water resources become more strained
item.description: Making waste water drinkable is a growing technology trend, especially in areas where limited water resources are restricting development.
item.content:encoded: Making waste water drinkable is a growing technology trend, especially in areas where limited water resources are restricting development.
item.dc:creator: Molly Duerig

item.title: 2025 saw relatively fewer natural disasters. Will you get a break on home insurance?
item.description: Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.
item.content:encoded:
Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.

(Image credit: ALLISON JOYCE/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Guam objects to Trump administration plan to allow deep-sea mining in western Pacific
item.description: The Trump administration wants to allow deep-sea mining for critical minerals near the American territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. Residents are worried about environmental impacts.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration wants to allow deep-sea mining for critical minerals near the American territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. Residents are worried about environmental impacts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Williams

item.title: These major issues have brought together Democrats and Republicans in states
item.description: Across the country, Republicans and Democrats have found bipartisan agreement on regulating artificial intelligence and data centers. But it's not just big tech aligning the two parties.
item.content:encoded:
Across the country, Republicans and Democrats have found bipartisan agreement on regulating artificial intelligence and data centers. But it's not just big tech aligning the two parties.

(Image credit: Ted Shaffrey)
item.dc:creator: Regan McCarthy

item.title: Why farmers in California are backing a giant solar farm
item.description: Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land … into solar farms.
item.content:encoded:
Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land … into solar farms.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Dan Charles
